rationale: Live-action UGC of a young man dancing in a dim room is a hard tonal mismatch for Block Blast: the game's DNA is calm, glossy, abstract puzzle satisfaction with no character presence, and the olive/earth palette plus nightclub lighting fights the royal-blue/navy/gold brand. The hook also fails to communicate the core line-clear loop in the first 3 seconds, which is critical for puzzle UA where gameplay-forward creatives dominate IPM. Audience skews younger student/late-night ('all night after school') vs. the 25-55 relaxation seeker — only partial overlap; recommend skipping unless reshot with a 25-45 talent reacting to actual gameplay B-roll cut-ins.
